tasks.description: Add route-unlock effect in EditorPaneTab.tsx. In EditorPaneComponent (after line 72, before the `if (isVideoEditorRoute(...))` guard at line 74), add a useEffect that unlocks the editor pane when on the video editor route. The effect must depend on BOTH `location.pathname` AND `isEditorPaneLocked` (hydration-safe pattern from useGenerationsPaneController.ts:234-238). Use `setIsEditorPaneLocked(false)` directly (stable ref) not `pane.toggleLock`. Guard with `&& isEditorPaneLocked` to avoid unnecessary calls. Both `isEditorPaneLocked` and `setIsEditorPaneLocked` are already destructured in useEditorPane() at lines 21-22 — surface them via the return value, or destructure from usePanes() directly in EditorPaneComponent. Import useEffect from React.

tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. `EditorPaneTab` adds the unlock-on-route effect before the early return, depends on both route and live lock state, and uses `setIsEditorPaneLocked(false)` directly.

tasks.description: Make PanesContext height calculation visibility-aware and use 70% when gens locked. In PanesContext.tsx, replace lines 140-170 with: (a) compute `editorFraction = locks.gens ? 0.7 : 0.9`, (b) compute `editorPaneHeight = Math.round(viewportHeight * editorFraction)`, (c) compute `bothVisible = editorVisible && generationsVisible` using the existing vars at lines 142-143, (d) only call `getEffectivePaneHeights` when `bothVisible`, otherwise use ideal heights directly. The existing `editorVisible`/`generationsVisible` vars are already computed but unused — wire them in. See plan Step 3a for exact code shape. Height trace: 1000px viewport + gens locked at 300px → ideal=700, 700+300=1000 ≤ 1000 → editor=700 (70%). Only editor locked on 1000px → ideal=900, no compression → 900.
tasks.executor_notes: Changed `PanesContext` so the ideal editor height is `70%` when `locks.gens` is true and `90%` otherwise, and gated compression behind `bothVisible`. Verified `layoutValue` now exposes the variable `editorPaneHeight` and effective heights flow from that branch.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. `PanesContext` now computes a 70% editor ideal only when generations is locked and only compresses heights when both panes are actually visible.

tasks.description: Update PanesContext.test.tsx for visibility-aware height changes. Change effectiveEditorHeight assertion from '300' to '540'. (2) Test at line 291-316 'effective heights remain stable across visibility toggle': this test is wrong post-fix — heights DO change when second pane opens. Rewrite to assert: initial (both closed, no compression) editor=540, gens=300; open editor alone → still 540,300 (no compression); open gens too → compression kicks in, editor=300, gens=300. (3) Add new test: 'uses 70% ideal when generations is locked' — on a 1200px viewport, lock gens, lock editor → ideal=840, 840+300=1140<1200 → tier 1: editor=840, gens=300. (4) Add new test: 'uses 90% ideal when generations is not locked' — lock editor only → effectiveEditorHeight=540 (90% of 600, no compression since gens not visible).

watch_items: EditorPaneTab line 138 uses position:fixed — after removing generationsOffset subtraction (T5), verify the pane still anchors correctly at top of viewport and doesn't overlap the generations pane visually

The hydration race: if usePaneLockPolicyState hydrates lock state asynchronously, the useEffect in T3 must re-fire when isEditorPaneLocked transitions from false→true after mount. The [location.pathname, isEditorPaneLocked] dep array handles this.
getEffectivePaneHeights is a pure function used by tests directly — the visibility gating must happen OUTSIDE it (in PanesProvider), not inside it, so the function signature stays the same

PanesContext test 'uses the ideal-fit tier when both panes fit within the viewport budget' (line 200) opens both gens and editor with small gens height — this should still work because bothVisible=true triggers compression path, and 540+60=600≤600 → tier 1

PanesContext test 'shrink-to-fit tier' (line 221) and 'keeps generations at configured height' (line 243) both open both panes — bothVisible=true, so compression path runs. These should pass unchanged.
PanesContext test 'degraded proportional split' (line 264) uses 250px viewport with both panes open — bothVisible=true, compression runs. Should pass unchanged.
LayoutMainContent still uses isGenerationsPaneLocked/isGenerationsPaneOpen/effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight for paddingBottom (line 41) — do NOT remove those from the destructuring
The 70% ideal applies only when locks.gens is true (DECISION-003). Hover-open (isGenerationsPaneOpen without lock) should still use 90% ideal.
Flag step_2_lacks_direct_regression_test is open/minor — no targeted test for the route-unlock effect. Acceptable risk given it follows the proven useGenerationsPaneController pattern.

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

**Parallelism:** T1, T3, T4 are independent and can execute in parallel. T2 depends on T1. T5 depends on T4 (needs context to handle compression before removing EditorPaneTab's local compensation). T3 and T5 both touch EditorPaneTab.tsx — if executing in parallel, merge carefully. T6 depends on T4.

**Critical ordering for EditorPaneTab.tsx:** T3 (add unlock effect) and T5 (remove generationsOffset) both modify this file. T3 adds code near line 73; T5 modifies lines 41-43 and the destructuring at lines 21-31. These touch different sections and won't conflict textually, but if done by separate agents, apply T3 first (it doesn't change the hook signature) then T5 (which removes variables from the hook).

**Test strategy for PanesContext (T6):** The hardest test rewrite is 'effective heights remain stable across visibility toggle' (line 291). The old test asserted heights DON'T change when toggling visibility — this was only true because getEffectivePaneHeights always ran and always compressed. Post-fix, heights WILL change: solo editor gets 540 (no compression), adding gens triggers compression to 300. Rewrite the test to assert this transition explicitly — it's the correct behavior.

**Height arithmetic cheat sheet (600px viewport, default gens=300px):**
- Both visible, gens NOT locked: ideal=540 (90%), 540+300=840>600 → tier 2: editor=300, gens=300
- Both visible, gens locked: ideal=420 (70%), 420+300=720>600 → tier 2: editor=300, gens=300  
- Editor only visible, gens NOT locked: ideal=540, no compression → editor=540
- Editor only visible, gens locked: ideal=420, no compression → editor=420
- For tier 1 verification, use 1200px viewport: both locked → ideal=840 (70%), 840+300=1140<1200 → editor=840, gens=300

**Watch for the `editorPaneHeight` ideal exposure:** The context exposes `editorPaneHeight` (ideal) to consumers. Post-fix this will be 70% or 90% depending on locks.gens. The PanesConsumer test helper reads this at data-testid='editorHeight'. The test at line 151 asserts '540' — this will still be true when gens is unlocked (default state). But the new 70%-ideal test needs a locked gens state where editorHeight would be 420 (70% of 600) or 840 (70% of 1200).

**Don't break the useEffect rules of hooks:** T3 adds a useEffect before an early return. React requires all hooks to be called unconditionally before any returns. The existing code has `useEditorPane()` call (which itself calls hooks) before the early return, so adding a useEffect there is fine — but it must be ABOVE the `if (isVideoEditorRoute) return null` line.
